head_title: Echoes of the Witch Wood · Archive

echoes_page.title: Echoes of the Witch Wood
echoes_page.subtitle: A solid and scalable action-platformer architecture, built on custom C++ systems and modular AI logic.
status_note: This project served as a fundamental testing ground for implementing core systems in C++.
Although development is suspended, the code produced for enemy AI and the save system represents some of my most structured work.

concept.title: Systems over Quantity
desc: The focus was on creating a robust framework for a two-character platformer.
Every system was designed to be actor-agnostic:
from portals to moving platforms, communication occurs via interfaces to ensure maximum maintainability.

tech_work.title: Core Systems Engineering
items: • AI & Enemy Framework: Developed a generic enemy base class with health management and a complex 'Kamikaze' AI, complete with State Machines (Chase, Attack, Stroll), Behavior Trees (Blackboard), and positional SFX.
• Save Architecture: Implementation of a complete data persistence pipeline based on interfaces, supporting checkpoints, multiple slots, and serialization of Player State and Game State.
• Advanced Movement Systems: Development of the Dash mechanic (Ground/Air) with conflict resolution on steep surfaces, and creation of a 'Floor Checking' system through C++ components and interfaces.
• Environmental Mechanics: Design of Spline-based moving platforms, timed platforms (falling platforms), and a portal system for instantaneous actor teleportation.
• Modular Audio System: Creation of a custom audio component to standardize sound variable management and simplify playback functions across the project.

lessons.title: Lessons Learned
desc: Through this project, I refined C++ Timer management for timed mechanics and Inheritance logic for AI.
Code cleanliness and the creation of a CodeMap were vital for coordinating movement systems with interaction systems (levers/doors).

highlights.title: Key Technical Contributions
items: ⁕ Enemy AI with State Machine and Blackboard
⁕ Hero & Ability Interface for decoupled systems

cta.title: Explore other projects
cta.subtitle: Even though this journey has ended, the systems created live on in my subsequent works.
